intro: Actinophloeus ambiguus, also known as the "Golondrina" or "Mora", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Actinophloeus ambiguus include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts.
